briefdescription.para: This function setups the interrupt system such that interrupts can occur for the CLK_WIZ device.

detaileddescription.para: This function is application specific since the actual system may or may not have an interrupt controller. The CLK_WIZ could be directly connected to a processor without an interrupt controller. The user should modify this function to fit the application.

ClkWizPtr
contains a pointer to the instance of the CLK_WIZ component which is going to be connected to the interrupt controller.
XST_SUCCESS if successful, otherwise XST_FAILURE.
None This function is the main entry point for the interrupt example using the XClk_Wiz driver. This function will set up the system with interrupts handlers.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE if the system setup failed.
XST_SUCCESS should never return since this function, if setup was successful, is blocking.

simplesect.para:
- In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.
If system setup was successful, this function is blocking in order to illustrate interrupt handling taking place for HPD events.

briefdescription.para: This function is the interrupt handler for the CLK_WIZ core.

detaileddescription.para: This handler reads the pending interrupt from the Interrupt Status register determines the source of the interrupts and calls the respective callbacks for the interrupts that are enabled in Interrupt Enable register and finally clears the interrupts.

The application is responsible for connecting this function to the interrupt system. Application beyond this core is also responsible for providing callbacks to handle interrupts and installing the callbacks using XClk_Wiz_SetCallBack() during initialization phase.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XClk_Wiz core instance.

simplesect.para: Interrupt should be enabled to execute interrupt handler.

briefdescription.para: XClk_Wiz_InterruptEnable will enable the interrupts present in the interrupt mask passed onto the function.

parameterdescription.para: is the XClk_Wiz instance to operate on
is the interrupt mask which need to be enabled in core

briefdescription.para: This is the Clk_Wiz_Reconfig function, it will reconfigure frequencies as per input array.

parameterdescription.para: provides pointer to clock wizard dynamic config
provides the index for Frequency divide register
provides the index for Frequency phase register

briefdescription.para: This is the Wait_For_Lock function, it will wait for lock to settle change frequency value.

parameterdescription.para: provides pointer to clock wizard dynamic config

briefdescription.para: This function is called when a clock out of range is received by the CLK_WIZ Subsystem core.

parameterdescription.para: contains a callback reference from the driver. In this case it is the instance pointer for the ClkWiz_Mon driver.
of interrupt which caused this event

briefdescription.para: This function is called when a clock glitch event is received by the CLK_WIZ Subsystem core.

parameterdescription.para: contains a callback reference from the driver. In this case it is the instance pointer for the ClkWiz_Mon driver.
of interrupt which caused this event

briefdescription.para: This function is called when a clock stop event is received by the CLK_WIZ Subsystem core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XClk_Wiz instance.
of interrupt which caused this event

briefdescription.para: For Microblaze we use an assembly loop that is roughly the same regardless of optimization level, although caches and memory access time can make the delay vary.

detaileddescription.para: Just keep in mind that after resetting or updating the PHY modes, the PHY typically needs time to recover.

briefdescription.para: This is the main function for XClk_Wiz interrupt example.

detaileddescription.para: If the ClkWiz_IntrExample function which sets up the system succeeds, this function will wait for the interrupts. Notify the events

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE if the interrupt example was unsuccessful.

simplesect.para: Unless setup failed, main will never return since ClkWiz_IntrExample is blocking (it is waiting on interrupts for Hot-Plug-Detect (HPD) events.

briefdescription.para: This file contains a design example using the XClk_Wiz driver with interrupts it will generate interrupt for clok glitch, clock overflow and underflow The user should have setup with 2 clocking wizard instances, one instance act as clocking monitor (Enable clock monitor in GUI), In another instance enable dynamic clock reconfiguration.

detaileddescription.para: In the present example XCLK_WIZ_DYN_DEVICE_ID assigned to clock wizard 1. Modify this value as per your dynamic clock reconfiguration Clocking wizard

This example requires an interrupt controller connected to the processor and the MIPI CLK_WIZ in the system. MODIFICATION HISTORY: Ver Who Date Changes 1.0 ram 2/12/16 Initial version for Clock Wizard 1.1 ms 01/23/17 Modified xil_printf statement in main function to ensure that "Successfully ran" and "Failed" strings are available in all examples. This is a fix for CR-965028. 1.6 sd 7/7/23 Add SDT support. 1.7 ml 11/15/23 Fix compilation errors reported with -std=c2x compiler flag 1.11 vlt 12/17/25 Update Doxygen comments to include SDT flow details.
